Title: Search Patient by exact family name token
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint /ws/fhir2/R4 reachable; OAuth/basic auth valid; at least one Patient with family=Smith exists
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate to FHIR endpoint | 2. GET /Patient?family=Smith | 3. Inspect returned Bundle | 4. Verify each entry resourceType=Patient and name.family matches
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; Bundle.type=searchset; every entry.resource.name.family contains Smith (case-insensitive starts-with per R4 string semantics); total reflects match count

Title: Search Patient by given name with :contains modifier
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint reachable; Patient with given containing 'ann' exists
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient?given:contains=ann | 2. Inspect Bundle entries | 3. Confirm substring match semantics
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; entries where given name contains substring 'ann' anywhere; if modifier unsupported returns OperationOutcome or ignores per server policy (document actual)

Title: Search Patient by identifier with system|value token
Preconditions: Patient registered with identifier system urn:oid OpenMRS ID
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient?identifier=http://openmrs.org/identifier|10001V | 2. Verify single/expected matches | 3. Confirm identifier.system and value match exactly
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; only Patients with matching identifier system AND value returned; no cross-system false positives

Title: Search Patient by identifier value only (no system)
Preconditions: Multiple identifier systems may share a value
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient?identifier=10001V | 2. Inspect results across systems
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; matches value across any system; results may include multiple systems sharing value

Title: Boundary - Patient birthdate equality vs prefix search
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient?birthdate=1990-01-01 | 2. GET /Patient?birthdate=ge1990-01-01&birthdate=le1990-12-31 | 3. Compare result sets
Expected_Result: Exact match returns only same-day; range with ge/le returns full-year set inclusively; boundary dates included

Title: Decision table - gender + active + birthdate combinations
Preconditions: Mixed dataset of active/inactive male/female/other patients
Test_Steps: 1. Execute combinations of gender in {male,female,other}, active in {true,false} | 2. Record returned counts | 3. Validate AND-combination logic
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; results satisfy logical AND of all params; no inactive returned when active=true; documented decision table holds

Title: Search any resource by _id returns exactly that resource
Preconditions: A known Patient logical id exists
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient?_id={uuid} | 2. Verify Bundle total=1 | 3. Verify entry.resource.id equals requested uuid
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; Bundle total=1; single entry with matching id; equivalent to read but wrapped in searchset

Title: Negative - search by non-existent _id returns empty searchset
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; Bundle.type=searchset; total=0; entry array empty or absent; NOT a 404

Title: Search by _lastUpdated with ge/le date range
Preconditions: Resources updated within known window exist
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Observation?_lastUpdated=ge2026-06-01&_lastUpdated=le2026-06-30 | 2. Verify meta.lastUpdated within range for all entries
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; every entry meta.lastUpdated >= start and <= end; resources outside window excluded

Title: Boundary - _lastUpdated with second/millisecond precision
Preconditions: Resource with precise lastUpdated timestamp known
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Observation?_lastUpdated=gt2026-06-15T10:00:00.000Z | 2. Validate timezone normalization and ms precision handling
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; comparison honors instant precision; timezone offsets normalized to UTC; no off-by-one at boundary

Title: Sort search results ascending by _lastUpdated
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient?_sort=_lastUpdated | 2. Verify ascending order of meta.lastUpdated across entries
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; entries ordered ascending by lastUpdated; ties stable

Title: Sort descending using -prefix and multi-key sort
Preconditions: Patients with varied family names and birthdates
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient?_sort=-birthdate,family | 2. Verify primary descending birthdate then secondary ascending family
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; primary key birthdate descending; secondary family ascending within ties

Title: Negative - sort by unsupported search param
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient?_sort=nonexistentParam | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: HTTP 400 with OperationOutcome (issue.code=not-supported or invalid) OR server ignores param and documents; no 500

Title: Limit page size with _count and verify Bundle entry count
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient?_count=5 | 2. Count entry resources (excluding OperationOutcome) | 3. Verify <=5
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; entry resource count <= 5; Bundle.total reflects full match set not page size

Title: Boundary - _count=0 returns count-only summary
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient?_count=0 | 2. Inspect Bundle
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; Bundle.total populated; no entry resources returned (count-only) per R4 spec; if unsupported document behavior

Title: Boundary - _count exceeding server max is capped
Preconditions: Server has documented max page size
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient?_count=100000 | 2. Verify returned page size capped to server max
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; page size capped at server maximum; next link present if more results; no error

Title: Negative - _count with non-numeric value
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient?_count=abc | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: HTTP 400; OperationOutcome issue.severity=error, code=invalid referencing _count

Title: Follow next link to retrieve second page
Preconditions: Result set larger than one page
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Observation?_count=10 | 2. Extract Bundle.link[relation=next].url | 3. GET next url | 4. Verify distinct entries, no duplicates
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; next link valid and resolvable; page 2 entries disjoint from page 1; self link present on each page

Title: State Transition - navigate first/self/next/previous links
Test_Steps: 1. Get page 1 (self,next) | 2. Follow next to page 2 (self,previous,next) | 3. Follow previous back to page 1 | 4. Compare contents
Expected_Result: Link relations consistent across transitions; previous from page2 equals page1 self contents; no entry loss or duplication across navigation

Title: Negative - stale/expired page link returns OperationOutcome
Preconditions: Server uses cached search results with TTL
Test_Steps: 1. Perform paged search | 2. Wait beyond cache TTL | 3. Follow stored next link
Expected_Result: HTTP 4xx with OperationOutcome OR fresh re-execution; no 500 server error; behavior documented

Title: Include referenced resource via _include
Preconditions: Observation referencing a Patient subject exists
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Observation?_include=Observation:subject&_count=5 | 2. Verify included Patient resources present | 3. Verify search.mode=include on those entries
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; matched Observations have entry.search.mode=match; referenced Patients have search.mode=include; references resolve

Title: Reverse include via _revinclude on Patient
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient?_id={uuid}&_revinclude=Observation:patient | 2. Verify Observations referencing patient included | 3. Check search.mode=include
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; Patient with search.mode=match; its Observations search.mode=include; only those referencing the patient included

Title: Pairwise - _include x _count x resource type combinations
Test_Steps: 1. Execute pairwise matrix of {Observation,Encounter,MedicationRequest} x _include target x _count {1,10} | 2. Validate include resolution each combo
Expected_Result: HTTP 200 each; included resources correct per type; _count limits match entries only (includes not counted against page) per R4

Title: Negative - _include with unsupported reference path
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Observation?_include=Observation:bogusref | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: HTTP 400 OperationOutcome (not-supported/invalid) OR include silently ignored; documented; no 500

Title: Chained search Observation by patient.name
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Observation?patient.name=Smith | 2. Verify returned Observations belong to patients named Smith
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; all Observations reference a Patient whose name matches Smith; chained join resolved server-side

Title: Typed chained search with reference type qualifier
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Encounter?subject:Patient.identifier=10001V | 2. Verify type-qualified chain resolves to Patient only
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; chain limited to Patient-typed subjects; identifier match enforced on referenced Patient

Title: Reverse chaining with _has parameter
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient?_has:Observation:patient:code=8867-4 | 2. Verify only patients with that Observation returned
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; only Patients referenced by an Observation with code 8867-4 returned; if _has unsupported return OperationOutcome (document)

Title: Token search Observation by code system|code
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Observation?code=http://loinc.org|8302-2 | 2. Verify code.coding system+code match
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; all entries have a coding with system http://loinc.org and code 8302-2; no other codes

Title: Token search with :not modifier excludes code
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Observation?code:not=http://loinc.org|8302-2 | 2. Verify excluded code absent
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; no entry has code 8302-2; if :not unsupported return OperationOutcome (document actual)

Title: Token search by code only without system
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Observation?code=8302-2 | 2. Verify matches across systems
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; matches code value 8302-2 regardless of system; broader than system|code form

Title: CodeableConcept :text modifier search
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Condition?code:text=hypertension | 2. Verify text/display match case-insensitive
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; matches on coding.display or text containing 'hypertension'; case-insensitive

Title: Date search with all prefixes eq/ne/gt/lt/ge/le/sa/eb
Preconditions: Encounters across a date spread exist
Test_Steps: 1. For each prefix issue GET /Encounter?date={prefix}2026-06-15 | 2. Record result sets | 3. Validate prefix semantics (sa=starts after, eb=ends before)
Expected_Result: HTTP 200 each; result sets honor prefix semantics; sa/eb correctly handle period boundaries; decision table consistent

Title: Boundary - date search with partial date precision (year only)
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Encounter?date=2026 | 2. Verify all 2026 encounters returned via implied range
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; partial date treated as full-year range per R4; all 2026-01-01..2026-12-31 matched

Title: Negative - malformed date value
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Encounter?date=2026-13-45 | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: HTTP 400; OperationOutcome issue.code=invalid referencing date param; no 500

Title: Quantity search with comparator and units
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Observation?value-quantity=gt70|http://unitsofmeasure.org|kg | 2. Verify value+unit comparison
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; matches Observations with valueQuantity > 70 kg in UCUM; unit system enforced

Title: Reference search by relative reference
Preconditions: Observations referencing a known Patient exist
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Observation?patient=Patient/{uuid} | 2. Verify subject reference matches
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; all entries subject/patient reference equals Patient/{uuid}

Title: Reference search by logical id only
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Observation?patient={uuid} | 2. Verify same results as typed reference
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; id-only reference resolves to Patient type; equivalent result set to Patient/{uuid}

Title: Composite search param component-code$component-value
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Observation?component-code-value-quantity=http://loinc.org|8480-6$gt140 | 2. Verify systolic>140 components
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; matches Observations whose component has code 8480-6 AND value>140 in same component; if unsupported OperationOutcome

Title: Search with _summary=count returns total only
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient?_summary=count | 2. Verify total present, no entries
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; Bundle.total populated; no entry resources; meta.tag SUBSETTED not needed for count

Title: Search with _elements returns subset and SUBSETTED tag
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient?_elements=name,gender | 2. Verify only requested elements plus mandatory; meta.tag SUBSETTED
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; resources contain name,gender + mandatory elements; meta.tag includes SUBSETTED; other fields omitted

Title: Unknown search parameter strict vs lenient handling
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient?bogusParam=x with Prefer:handling=strict | 2. Repeat with Prefer:handling=lenient | 3. Compare
Expected_Result: Strict: HTTP 400 OperationOutcome rejecting unknown param; Lenient: param ignored, HTTP 200; behavior matches Prefer header

Title: Negative - unsupported modifier on search param
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient?name:exact=Smith where :exact unsupported for that param | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: HTTP 400 OperationOutcome (not-supported) OR documented graceful handling; no 500

Title: Negative - search with conflicting AND params on same field
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient?gender=male&gender=female | 2. Inspect logical AND result
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; AND semantics yield empty set (no patient both genders); not an error; total=0

Title: OR semantics with comma-separated values
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient?gender=male,female | 2. Verify union of male and female
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; OR semantics; returns patients matching male OR female

Title: Instance read returns current version with ETag
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient/{uuid} | 2. Inspect ETag and meta.versionId | 3. Verify Last-Modified header
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; resource returned; ETag matches meta.versionId; Last-Modified equals meta.lastUpdated

Title: vread retrieves specific historical version
Preconditions: Patient updated at least once (>=2 versions)
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient/{uuid}/_history | 2. Pick prior versionId | 3. GET /Patient/{uuid}/_history/{versionId} | 4. Verify content of that version
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; returned resource matches the historical version; meta.versionId equals requested; immutable snapshot

Title: Conditional read with If-None-Match returns 304
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient/{uuid} capture ETag | 2. GET /Patient/{uuid} with If-None-Match: {etag} | 3. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 304 Not Modified; empty body; bandwidth saved; if unsupported full 200 (document)

Title: Negative - read deleted resource returns 410 Gone
Test_Steps: 1. DELETE /Patient/{uuid} | 2. GET /Patient/{uuid} | 3. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 410 Gone with OperationOutcome; or 404 per server; history still accessible via _history

Title: Negative - read non-existent id returns 404 OperationOutcome
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient/nonexistent-uuid | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: HTTP 404; OperationOutcome issue.code=not-found; no stack trace leakage

Title: Resource type-level _history returns versioned bundle
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient/_history | 2. Inspect Bundle.type=history | 3. Verify entries ordered newest-first with request method
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; Bundle.type=history; entries include request.method (POST/PUT/DELETE) and response; reverse chronological

Title: Instance _history with _since filter
Preconditions: Patient with multiple versions across time
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient/{uuid}/_history?_since=2026-06-01T00:00:00Z | 2. Verify only versions after _since
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; only versions with lastUpdated >= _since returned; older versions excluded

Title: State Transition - create then update then delete history chain
Preconditions: Write access to a test resource
Test_Steps: 1. POST create resource | 2. PUT update | 3. DELETE | 4. GET /_history | 5. Verify version chain v1 create, v2 update, v3 delete
Expected_Result: versionId increments 1->2->3; history shows create,update,delete in order; vread of v1/v2 still retrievable; current read returns 410

Title: Conditional create with If-None-Exist no match creates
Preconditions: No Patient with identifier TEST-CC-1 exists
Test_Steps: 1. POST /Patient with If-None-Exist: identifier=...|TEST-CC-1 | 2. Verify 201 Created | 3. Capture location
Expected_Result: HTTP 201 Created; new Patient created; Location header with id; resource searchable by identifier

Title: Conditional create with single existing match returns 200 no-op
Preconditions: Exactly one Patient matches identifier TEST-CC-1
Test_Steps: 1. POST /Patient with If-None-Exist matching one existing | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 200 OK; no new resource created; existing returned/referenced; idempotent

Title: Negative - conditional create with multiple matches returns 412
Preconditions: Two Patients share identifier value used in criteria
Test_Steps: 1. POST /Patient with If-None-Exist matching >1 | 2. Inspect status
Expected_Result: HTTP 412 Precondition Failed with OperationOutcome; no resource created; ambiguity reported

Title: Conditional update with criteria matching one updates it
Test_Steps: 1. PUT /Patient?identifier=...|TEST-CU-1 with updated body | 2. Verify matched resource updated, versionId incremented
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; matched resource updated in place; versionId++; id unchanged

Title: Conditional update no match creates new resource
Test_Steps: 1. PUT /Patient?identifier=...|NEW-CU with body lacking id | 2. Verify creation
Expected_Result: HTTP 201 Created; new resource created from criteria+body; server-assigned id

Title: Negative - conditional update body id conflicts with match
Preconditions: One Patient matches but body.id differs from matched id
Test_Steps: 1. PUT /Patient?identifier=...|X with body.id=different | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: HTTP 400 OperationOutcome; conflict rejected; no silent id reassignment

Title: Version-aware update with If-Match optimistic locking
Test_Steps: 1. GET resource capture ETag | 2. PUT with If-Match: {etag} matching current | 3. Verify success | 4. PUT again with stale ETag
Expected_Result: First PUT 200 with versionId++; second PUT with stale If-Match returns 409 Conflict/412; lost-update prevented

Title: Search response is well-formed searchset Bundle
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient?_count=3 | 2. Validate Bundle.type=searchset, total, link.self, entry.fullUrl, entry.search.mode
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; Bundle.type=searchset; each entry has fullUrl and search.mode=match; self link present; total numeric

Title: Transaction bundle with POST/PUT and internal references
Test_Steps: 1. POST / (base) a Bundle.type=transaction containing POST Patient (urn:uuid:p1) and POST Observation referencing urn:uuid:p1 | 2. Inspect response bundle
Test_Data: transaction bundle: Patient + Observation w/ urn:uuid ref
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; response Bundle.type=transaction-response; all entries 201; urn:uuid resolved to real Patient id in Observation.subject; atomic

Title: Negative - transaction atomic rollback on one failing entry
Test_Steps: 1. POST transaction Bundle with one valid Patient and one invalid Observation (bad code) | 2. Inspect response | 3. Verify Patient NOT persisted
Expected_Result: HTTP 4xx; transaction-response shows failure; NO entries persisted (all-or-nothing); Patient not searchable afterward

Title: Batch bundle entries processed independently
Test_Steps: 1. POST batch Bundle with one valid create and one invalid create | 2. Inspect response per-entry status
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; Bundle.type=batch-response; valid entry 201, invalid entry 4xx; NO rollback of valid entry (independent)

Title: Batch bundle with GET search entries
Test_Steps: 1. POST batch Bundle containing GET Patient?family=Smith and GET Observation?code=8302-2 | 2. Inspect nested searchset responses
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; each entry.resource is a nested searchset Bundle; entry.response.status 200; independent results

Title: Transaction with conditional create inside bundle
Preconditions: Write access; identifier TX-CC not present
Test_Steps: 1. POST transaction Bundle with entry POST Patient + ifNoneExist on identifier | 2. Repeat same bundle | 3. Verify second is no-op
Expected_Result: First run creates; second run no-op (200) due to conditional; idempotent transaction; no duplicate Patient

Title: Negative - circular references in transaction bundle
Test_Steps: 1. POST transaction with two entries referencing each other via urn:uuid | 2. Inspect handling
Expected_Result: HTTP 200 if server resolves placeholders in two passes, else 422 OperationOutcome; no infinite loop or 500

Title: Patient $everything returns compartment bundle
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient/{uuid}/$everything | 2. Verify Bundle includes Patient + related resources in compartment
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; searchset Bundle; includes Patient and clinical resources (Observation,Encounter,Condition,MedicationRequest) in patient compartment

Title: Patient $everything with _since and _type filters
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient/{uuid}/$everything?_type=Observation,Condition&_since=2026-06-01 | 2. Verify only those types after date
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; only specified types included; only resources updated since date; Patient may still be included

Title: Negative - $everything on unauthorized patient compartment
Preconditions: User scoped to one patient/location only
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate as restricted user | 2. GET /Patient/{otherPatientUuid}/$everything | 3. Inspect response
Expected_Result: HTTP 403 Forbidden OR empty compartment; no PHI of other patient leaked; access denial audited

Title: Validate resource against profile via $validate
Test_Steps: 1. POST /Patient/$validate with a Patient body | 2. Inspect OperationOutcome | 3. Repeat with invalid body (bad gender code)
Expected_Result: Valid body returns OperationOutcome with information/no errors; invalid returns issues with error severity and location

Title: Observation $lastn returns most recent per code
Preconditions: Patient has multiple Observations per code
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Observation/$lastn?patient={uuid}&category=vital-signs&max=1 | 2. Verify latest per code group
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; returns most recent N Observations grouped by code; if unsupported OperationOutcome (document)

Title: ValueSet $expand returns concepts
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ValueSet/{id}/$expand | 2. Verify expansion.contains concepts with system+code
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; ValueSet with expansion.contains; each concept has system, code, display; if no terminology svc OperationOutcome

Title: OperationOutcome structure on validation error
Test_Steps: 1. POST /Patient with malformed JSON | 2. Inspect OperationOutcome
Expected_Result: HTTP 400; resourceType=OperationOutcome; issue[].severity=error, code, diagnostics; no internal stack trace exposed

Title: OperationOutcome issue.expression points to bad field
Test_Steps: 1. POST /Patient with invalid gender value | 2. Inspect issue.expression/location
Expected_Result: HTTP 400/422; issue.expression or location identifies Patient.gender; severity=error; actionable diagnostics

Title: US Core Patient profile mandatory elements present
Preconditions: US Core IG profiled server or validation expectations
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient/{uuid} | 2. Verify identifier, name, gender present | 3. Validate against us-core-patient profile
Expected_Result: Patient includes US Core mandatory: identifier, name, gender; meta.profile may assert us-core-patient; passes profile validation

Title: US Core race/ethnicity extension present and coded
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient/{uuid} | 2. Locate us-core-race and us-core-ethnicity extensions | 3. Validate ombCategory codes
Expected_Result: Extensions present with valid ombCategory Coding (system urn:oid:2.16.840.1.113883.6.238) and text; passes US Core

Title: US Core Observation vital-signs category and LOINC code
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Observation?category=vital-signs&patient={uuid} | 2. Verify category coding + LOINC code + valueQuantity UCUM
Expected_Result: Observations have category=vital-signs (system terminology.hl7.org), LOINC code, valueQuantity with UCUM unit; US Core vitals compliant

Title: CapabilityStatement advertises supported resources and params
Test_Steps: 1. GET /metadata | 2. Verify CapabilityStatement.fhirVersion=4.0.1 | 3. Confirm rest.resource entries and searchParam list
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; CapabilityStatement; fhirVersion 4.0.1; lists Patient/Observation/etc with interactions and searchParams; format json+xml

Title: Declared search params actually function (conformance gap)
Test_Steps: 1. Parse searchParams for Patient from /metadata | 2. Execute each declared param with sample value | 3. Flag any returning 400 not-supported
Expected_Result: Every param declared in CapabilityStatement returns 200 (not not-supported); conformance gaps flagged as defects

Title: Format negotiation via _format and Accept header
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient/{uuid}?_format=xml | 2. GET with Accept: application/fhir+json | 3. Verify content-type matches
Expected_Result: XML request returns application/fhir+xml; JSON Accept returns application/fhir+json; same logical content both formats

Title: Negative - unsupported Accept media type
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient/{uuid} with Accept: application/pdf | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: HTTP 406 Not Acceptable OR falls back to default fhir+json per server policy; documented; no 500

Title: Negative - unauthenticated FHIR request rejected
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient without auth header | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: HTTP 401 Unauthorized; WWW-Authenticate header present; OperationOutcome; no PHI returned

Title: SMART scope enforcement patient/Observation.read
Test_Steps: 1. Obtain token with scope patient/Observation.read only | 2. GET /Observation (allowed) | 3. POST /Observation (write) | 4. GET /MedicationRequest
Expected_Result: Read Observation 200; write Observation 403; read MedicationRequest 403; scopes enforced per resource and operation

Title: Search param injection attempt is neutralized
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Patient?family=Smith'%20OR%201=1-- | 2. GET /Patient?name=<script>alert(1)</script> | 3. Inspect responses
Test_Data: SQLi and XSS payloads in params
Expected_Result: No SQL injection (parameterized); no reflected script; HTTP 200 empty/safe or 400; payload not executed/echoed unescaped

Title: No PHI leaked in OperationOutcome diagnostics
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger validation errors with PHI-laden body | 2. Inspect OperationOutcome diagnostics for echoed PHI
Expected_Result: Diagnostics describe error without echoing sensitive PHI values verbatim; no SSN/MRN leakage in error text

Title: FHIR write generates audit/history record
Preconditions: DB access or audit log access; write performed
Test_Steps: 1. POST /Observation | 2. Query audit/history store (fhir2 mappings / openmrs audit) | 3. Verify record with user, timestamp, action
Expected_Result: Audit/history row exists for the write with acting user, action=create, timestamp, resource id; immutable

Title: FHIR reference integrity to backing data model
Preconditions: DB access; Observation with subject reference
Test_Steps: 1. Read Observation via FHIR | 2. Resolve subject Patient/{uuid} | 3. Cross-check backing person/obs tables match uuid
Expected_Result: FHIR reference uuid maps to existing backing record; no dangling reference; round-trip read returns same patient

Title: Swagger/OpenAPI docs accessible and keyboard navigable
Test_Steps: 1. Open FHIR API docs page | 2. Navigate via keyboard only | 3. Run axe scan for WCAG AA
Expected_Result: Docs reachable by keyboard; focus visible; axe reports no critical WCAG 2.1 AA violations on docs UI

Title: Search with deep paging maintains response SLA
Test_Steps: 1. Execute paged search to page 50 via next links | 2. Measure latency each page | 3. Verify within SLA
Expected_Result: Each page returns within SLA (e.g., <2s p95); no progressive degradation; cursor/offset paging stable

Title: Exploratory - combine _include + _revinclude + chained + _sort
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Encounter?patient.name=Smith&_include=Encounter:patient&_revinclude=Observation:encounter&_sort=-date | 2. Explore correctness and edge behavior
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; matched Encounters sorted desc by date; included Patients and revincluded Observations present with correct search.mode; document anomalies

Title: HL7 v2 ADT ingest reflected in FHIR Patient
Preconditions: HL7 v2 inbound channel mapped to FHIR (platform/integration engine)
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A04 with PID | 2. GET /Patient?identifier matching PID-3 | 3. Verify mapped fields
Expected_Result: Registered patient retrievable via FHIR with name/dob/gender mapped from PID; identifier preserved; if no engine mark Platform

Title: HL7 v2 ORU result maps to FHIR Observation
Preconditions: HL7 v2 ORU pipeline to FHIR
Test_Steps: 1. Send ORU^R01 with OBX numeric result | 2. GET /Observation?patient&code | 3. Verify value/unit/code mapping
Expected_Result: Observation created with LOINC code, valueQuantity, unit, status mapped from OBX; subject linked; document gaps if no pipeline

Title: API - MedicationRequest search by status and intent
Test_Steps: 1. GET /MedicationRequest?status=active&intent=order&patient={uuid} | 2. Verify filter combination
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; all entries status=active AND intent=order for patient; other statuses excluded

Title: Boundary - search returns empty when no resource of type supported
Preconditions: A resource type not implemented by server
Test_Steps: 1. GET /MolecularSequence (likely unsupported) | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: HTTP 404 OperationOutcome (resource type not supported) OR 200 empty per server; documented; consistent with CapabilityStatement

Title: Conditional delete by search criteria
Preconditions: One resource matches criteria; delete enabled
Test_Steps: 1. DELETE /Observation?code=...|TESTDEL&patient={uuid} | 2. Verify matched resource deleted | 3. GET returns 410/404
Expected_Result: HTTP 200/204; single matched resource deleted; subsequent read 410 Gone; if multiple match server returns 412 (separate case)

Title: JSON Patch partial update of a resource
Test_Steps: 1. PATCH /Patient/{uuid} with application/json-patch+json replacing gender | 2. Verify only gender changed, versionId++
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; only targeted element changed; other fields intact; versionId incremented; if unsupported 4xx documented
